commentary_text: The market assigns Portugal a 51% win probability versus the model's 35%, a 16-percentage-point gap favoring Portugal that dominates the forecast divergence. Sportsbooks and crypto traders show near-identical conviction on this outcome, suggesting real-money flows have converged on similar information. The model sees an essentially three-way contest; the market sees a Portugal lean with material edge. This gap could reflect recent form, lineup availability, or injury status unknown to the model's last update—factors that would sharpen conviction around one side without necessarily changing the fundamental competitive balance. The dashboard flags this as a signal worth monitoring rather than a resolution of which view will prove correct.
